body_txt: Political Overcorrectness —language policing by socially liberal Straw Characters who baselessly accuse things of being bigoted, have Insistent Terminology for every demographic, and nitpick everyone else's speech—is largely Played for Laughs . They may be annoying or misguided, but they have noble intentions and aren't really hurting anyone. Here, things go a bit further than that, and the threat posed by the "PC police" is No Mere Windmill . Politically correct Moral Guardians are the antagonists of a work, or their beliefs and actions are shown to be outright harmful to society. Needless to say, this is a very blatant Take That! and a strong sign that a work may be an Author Tract . Whether the problems are with the advocates themselves or their beliefs may vary. On a personal level, they may be vindictive Jerkasses who have no problem harassing and ostracizing others—whether it's because they believe in doing wrong for the right reasons , they're Hypocrites with a bad case of Moral Myopia , or Not-So-Well-Intentioned Extremists who just want a socially permissible reason to hurt and bully others. On a more conceptual level, political correctness could be shown to psychologically harm its practitioners, such as turning people into coddled Manchildren who can't handle controversy, or making them miserable and terrified of free expression for fear of offending others. At worst, it may have totalitarian overtones or undergo a Persecution Flip and oppress anything arbitrarily deemed to be bigoted (too straight, too white, too male, too Christian, too conservative, too patriotic, etc.). Its followers are Obliviously Evil at best and Dirty Communists at worst. The harsher sister trope to Political Overcorrectness . Compare The War on Straw , Politically Incorrect Hero , Culture Police , Thoughtcrime , and Newspeak . Contrast Angry White Man and Politically Incorrect Villain . Because this is the subject of many contentious political debates, In-Universe Examples Only please!
